name: azure-storage-queue-py
description: Azure Queue Storage SDK for Python. Use for reliable message queuing, task distribution, and asynchronous processing.
capabilities: Create, configure, and manage Azure Queue Storage queues and messages
Send and receive messages with visibility timeout and TTL control
Implement async message processing with aio clients
Handle message encoding with Base64 binary support
Monitor queues with peek operations without affecting visibility
Manage message lifecycle with update and delete operations
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Prompt-only skill containing documentation and code examples for Azure Queue Storage SDK. No executable code or scripts present. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 suspicious patterns detected. All content is educational reference material for legitimate Azure SDK usage. No security concerns identified.
valueStatement: Implement reliable message queuing and asynchronous processing with Azure Queue Storage SDK. This skill provides production-ready patterns for task distribution, background job processing, and decoupled application communication.
userTitle: Manage Azure Queue Storage in Python
limitations: Does not execute code - provides examples and patterns only
Requires valid Azure Storage account and authentication setup
Cannot handle advanced messaging features like topics or sessions
Examples assume Python environment with azure-storage-queue installed

useCases.targetUser: Backend developers building scalable web applications
useCases.title: Background Task Processing
useCases.description: Queue long-running tasks for asynchronous processing by worker processes. Ideal for web applications that need to offload heavy computations or batch jobs.

useCases.title: Microservices Communication
useCases.description: Enable reliable message-based communication between microservices. Decouple services with queues for fault-tolerant asynchronous messaging.

useCases.targetUser: Data engineers building ETL and batch processing pipelines
useCases.title: Job Queue Management
useCases.description: Implement distributed task scheduling and job queue systems. Manage work distribution across multiple workers with visibility timeout control.

promptTemplates.title: Create a Queue
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me create an Azure Queue Storage queue named 'tasks' using QueueServiceClient. Show me the authentication setup with DefaultAzureCredential.
promptTemplates.scenario: Setting up a new queue for task processing

promptTemplates.title: Send Messages with Options
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to send a message to 'tasks' queue with a 5-minute visibility timeout and 24-hour time-to-live. The message content should be JSON.
promptTemplates.scenario: Queuing a task with delayed visibility and expiration

promptTemplates.title: Process Messages Reliably
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a function that receives up to 10 messages, processes each one, and deletes them only after successful processing. Handle errors properly.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building a reliable message consumer with error handling

promptTemplates.title: Async Queue Operations
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert this queue processing code to use async QueueClient from azure.storage.queue.aio. Show me the async context manager pattern.

bestPractices: Always delete messages after successful processing to prevent reprocessing and queue clutter
Set visibility timeout based on actual processing time plus a safety margin
Monitor dequeue_count to detect and handle poison messages before they consume resources
Use peek_messages for monitoring and debugging without affecting message visibility
antiPatterns: Receiving messages without deleting them after processing causes duplicate work and queue bloat
Setting visibility timeout too short causes messages to reappear before processing completes
Ignoring dequeue_count leads to infinite retries on malformed or unprocessable messages
Using receive_messages for monitoring when peek_messages is the correct tool

faq.question: What is the difference between Queue Storage and Service Bus?
faq.answer: Queue Storage is simpler and cost-effective for basic message queuing. Service Bus provides advanced features like topics, sessions, and transactions. Use Queue Storage for simple task queues.

faq.question: How do I handle authentication securely?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential from azure-identity. It supports multiple authentication methods and automatically picks the right one based on your environment.

faq.question: What happens if I forget to delete a message?
faq.answer: The message becomes visible again after the visibility timeout expires. It will be reprocessed, potentially causing duplicate work. Always delete after successful processing.

faq.question: Can I send binary data in messages?
faq.answer: Yes. Use BinaryBase64EncodePolicy and BinaryBase64DecodePolicy when creating the QueueClient to handle binary messages automatically.

faq.question: How many messages can I retrieve at once?
faq.answer: Use the messages_per_page parameter in receive_messages. The maximum is 32 messages per batch. For larger volumes, make multiple calls.

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with async Python?
faq.answer: Yes. Import from azure.storage.queue.aio for the async client. All operations have async versions (await client.send_message, async for in receive_messages).
